title: Iran submits second peace proposal through Pakistani mediators as US-Iran tensions persist
summary: Iran submitted a new peace proposal through Pakistani diplomatic channels, representing a second round of negotiations after the Trump administration rejected a previous offer that would have delayed nuclear program discussions.
contents:
## Iran Offers New Peace Terms Through Pakistani Diplomatic Channel

### Diplomatic Development
Iran submitted a new peace proposal through Pakistani mediators, marking the second attempt at diplomatic resolution of the ongoing US-Iran military confrontation. Pakistan continues to serve as a key intermediary between the two adversaries.

### Proposal Details
According to intelligence analysis, the Iranian proposal reportedly includes:
- Reopening the Strait of Hormuz to commercial shipping
- Establishing a framework for phased de-escalation
- Deferring nuclear program restrictions to a later negotiation phase

### US Response
The Trump administration rejected a previous Iranian offer that would have delayed discussions on Iran's nuclear program. President Trump maintains that any deal must include comprehensive nuclear restrictions. The administration faces simultaneous pressure from the War Powers Resolution deadline.

### Intelligence Assessment
The second proposal signals Tehran's desire to avoid further escalation while maintaining leverage through the Strait of Hormuz closure. Military analysts note that both sides continue positioning forces despite diplomatic overtures, suggesting preparations for either outcome.

### Pakistani Mediation Role
Pakistan's role as intermediary leverages its geographic proximity and historical diplomatic relationships with both nations. Pakistani officials facilitated the transmission of proposals through established back-channel communications.
